scenes.title: Opening
scenes.editIntent: Title card. Frame the category fast: finance, analytics, profitability, AI-assisted insight. The promise before the demo — static reports are the old world, operational financial intelligence is the new one.

blocks.text: Finance teams run on static reports.
Variance analysis takes hours.
This is what operational financial intelligence looks like.

scenes.title: Command Center
scenes.editIntent: The hero slide. Financial Command Center at rest: four KPI cards (Revenue up, Margin down, Operating Profit down, YoY +5.9%), three segment health cards (Retail AT RISK, Enterprise HEALTHY, Online STABLE), variance summary column, and the AI Analyst terminal bottom-right. Sets the complete picture before zooming into the problem.

blocks.text: Acme Corp. Three business segments.
Revenue is up. But margin is under pressure.
Retail is the problem. The system already knows.

scenes.title: Variance Detected
scenes.editIntent: The variance breaks. Gross Margin down 2.1 points vs last year. Operating Profit -$120K. The system detected it instantly and surfaces a full driver table: Price, Volume, Labor Cost, Mix Effect, Discounting — each with LY vs Current vs Delta. Severity: HIGH. This is the MSP incident equivalent.

blocks.text: Gross margin just declined 2.1 points versus last year.
Operating profit is down $120K.
The system has already decomposed the drivers.

scenes.title: Root Cause
scenes.editIntent: The AI analyst's diagnosis. Three primary drivers: labor cost inflation (+8%), product mix shift toward lower-margin offerings, discounting increase in NE region. Margin last year was 35.9%, now 33.8% — 2.1 point compression explained. AI notes panel on the right. This is the finance equivalent of MSP root cause.

blocks.text: Three drivers. Labor, mix, and discounting.
Labor is rising faster than revenue.
The mix shift explains the rest.

scenes.title: Profitability Mix
scenes.editIntent: Profitability mix analysis. Left: mix comparison table — four service categories, last period vs current share, margin quality rating. Staffing Services share fell from 42% to 39% (Low quality). Consulting rose 28→31% (High). Right: the driver tree — Revenue split into Price +140K, Volume +170K, Mix -60K, with the child node showing low-margin share increasing. This is the finance causality graph.

blocks.text: The mix is the story.
More revenue — from lower-margin work.
That's what compressed the gross margin.

scenes.title: YoY Comparison
scenes.editIntent: Credibility slide. Year-over-year KPI table: Revenue +5.9%, Gross Margin -2.1 pts, Operating Profit -12.9%, Avg Discount Rate +1.1 pts. Below it: the profit bridge waterfall — starting from $0.93M last year, adding price and volume impacts, subtracting labor, mix shift, and discounting to land at $0.81M current. Horizontal bars animate left-to-right.

blocks.text: Revenue up. Profit down.
The bridge shows exactly how we got here.
Price and volume helped. Labor and mix hurt more.

scenes.title: Action Playbook
scenes.editIntent: Recovery in action. Four-step margin-recovery playbook: reduce discounting 2%, shift mix toward high-margin services, reallocate labor to top-yield accounts, tighten pricing discipline in weak regions. Projected outcome: Margin +1.4 pts, Operating Profit +$95K. Before/After comparison cards: Margin 33.8% → 35.2%, Op Profit $0.81M → $0.905M. This is the finance equivalent of MSP playbook execution.

blocks.text: Four actions. Specific, measurable, immediately executable.
Margin recovers 1.4 points.
Operating profit up $95K — without touching revenue targets.

scenes.title: Before vs After
scenes.editIntent: The closing argument. Before: static monthly reports, manual variance analysis, disconnected spreadsheets, lagging insight, hard to simulate. After: live diagnostic environment, instant driver identification, unified operating view, action-ready intelligence, built-in scenario modeling. Bottom tagline: Time to insight: Hours → Seconds. This is not reporting. This is financial operations.

blocks.text: This is the difference.
Not a better dashboard. A financial operator.
You are not running reports anymore. You are running the business.
